type: PROPOSE

note: My task: add return_positions param to Encoding.encode in tiktoken/core.py to track token start char positions. I only need core.py.

type: PROPOSE

note: My task (Add Token Transformation) only touches tiktoken/core.py - adding a transformers param to encode(). Let me know your files so we can avoid collisions; if your feature also needs core.py we should split by function/region.

type: REVISE

your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our features need core.py (mine: return_positions param on Encoding.encode; yours: transformers param on Encoding.encode) - true disjoint file split isn't possible since we both touch the same method signature. Proposal: we both edit core.py but keep changes localized/additive - I will only add a new  kwarg to  (appended after your params, not touching your lines) plus its return-value branch. Please do the same for your  param (append yours, don't restructure the whole method). That way our diffs are additive and low-conflict-risk even though the file is shared. Let me know if that works and I'll start on my ACCEPT.

note: Both our features need core.py (mine: return_positions param on Encoding.encode; yours: transformers param on Encoding.encode) - true disjoint file split is not possible since we both touch the same method signature. That way our diffs are additive and low-conflict-risk even though the file is shared. Let me know if that works and I will send ACCEPT.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py (encode() signature + top-of-body text preprocessing only)
your_files: tiktoken/core.py (encode() return-statements + encode_ordinary() only)
note: We both need core.py's encode(). To avoid clobbering: I'll only touch the encode() signature (adding transformers=...) and insert a small block at the TOP of encode()'s body (right after the docstring, before 'if allowed_special == "all":') that applies transformers to text. I will NOT touch the return statements or encode_ordinary(). Please add return_positions by editing the signature (I'll add my param too, so append yours after mine) and the return statements / encode_ordinary logic. Let's each go slow and re-check with coop-recv before writing, to serialize actual edits. OK?
